Specimen information

Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated serum creatinine), with proteinuria and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, bilirubin), suggesting possible renal or urinary tract pathology. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low—may indicate analytical error or altered red cell turnover. No evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Lipid profile is within target. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, urine abnormalities, and review of HbA1c result in clinical context.
